description: Accounts in Arabic script and Greek/Coptic numerals. Very neat. Mentions a Jewish man named Fayrūz many times, and at one point he is called "al-Qaraʾ al-Qāhirī (the Qaraite Jew from (New) Cairo). The main commodities mentioned are cloaks (milḥafa) and flax/linen (kattān) sometimes together with the adjective musarraj(?). Also mentions the amounts still owed to the wife of Ibn al-Musallaḥ. The term 'mutajaddid' (renewed?) appears several times. (Information in part from Goitein's index card.)
description_he: חשבונות בערבית ובספרות יווניות/קופטיות; מזכיר פיירוז, יהודי קראי מקהיר; סחורות: גלימות ופשתן; מזכיר חובות לאשת אבן אל-מסלח
